Family care comes with
a hidden second job.

For everyone.

For you

You're managing your condition, your supports, and the stress of plan reviews.

For your people

They show up because they love you. They quietly give more than anyone sees. They deserve more than anyone asks.

Meet Mallee

You just have to
answer the phone.

01

Mallee calls

Twice a week, Mallee calls your support person. Not with a form to fill in. Not with a system to navigate. Just a gentle five minute conversation, the kind that feels like talking to someone who actually gets it.

02

Mallee listens

Mallee listens to what the week looked like. What got managed. What was harder. What your people helped with. And while you're just talking, Mallee is quietly building your Care Record.

Example check-in question
"How many hours did you spend helping with personal care this week, things like bathing, dressing, or getting around the home?"
03

Mallee builds

The evidence your plan review needs, the documentation your Allied Health team can use, all of it organised and ready, without anyone needing to prepare, remember, or explain the system to anyone. No apps, no downloads, no training, no typing required.

What Mallee builds

This is what your
Care Record looks like.

After 13 weeks of gentle check-ins, here's the picture Mallee builds — automatically, in the language your NDIS assessor needs.

Support Person Relationship Primary Activities Avg Hrs/Week
Adaeze Okafor Spouse Personal care, medication management, meal preparation, transport to appointments 14.5 hrs
Ruth Okafor Mother Domestic assistance, emotional support, school pickup when Adaeze at appointments 5.0 hrs
Daniel Okafor Brother Home maintenance, transport, social outings 3.0 hrs
Priya Sharma Neighbour / Friend Ad hoc transport, grocery assistance, welfare check-ins 1.5 hrs
Notable Check-in Observations
Extracted from 26 conversations
  • 14 Nov 2025 Adaeze noted support hours increased to approximately 22 hours that week. No formal support was available during this period and Adaeze managed the additional tasks without assistance.
  • 31 Oct 2025 James reported confidence managing communication with his GP has improved since Adaeze began attending appointments. Information retention remains a challenge and Adaeze provides verbal recap support.
  • 9 Oct 2025 Ruth took over primary domestic support for two weeks while Adaeze managed a family obligation. Transition was managed without formal respite. Coordination required significant time from James to brief Ruth on routines.
  • 18 Sept 2025 James noted he had declined two social invitations due to transport uncertainty. Daniel has begun providing a regular Sunday outing which James describes as important to his sense of normalcy.
I-CAN Domain Coverage
Informal support evidenced across domains
Daily Activities
Communication
Social & Community
Home & Living
Learning & Work

Informal support documented across 4 of 5 I-CAN functional domains. Evidence is sufficient to support assessment across Daily Activities, Communication, Social & Community Participation, and Home & Living.

For assessors: This Care Record was generated by Mallee through structured fortnightly voice check-ins with the participant's informal support network. All data reflects self-reported responses to structured check-in questions and does not constitute clinical assessment or diagnosis. Check-in transcripts are available on request.

How it works

Here to help.
Here's how.

Your family can be family, not just your care team When the documentation takes care of itself, the relationship can go back to being what it was, before the NDIS made it complicated.
Your Care Record builds itself in the background Every conversation adds to the picture. By the time your I-CAN assessment arrives, you have months of structured evidence. Not a memory test.
A gentle way to check in with your support network Mallee calls your people. Two minutes, twice a week. They talk, Mallee listens, and everything that matters gets captured without anyone having to write a single thing down.
Who it's for

You'll recognise yourself
in one of these.

01

The daughter driving her mum to appointments twice a week, fitting it around work and her own kids, with no time to document any of it.

02

The person managing a progressive condition who needs the NDIS to understand what their life actually looks like, not what they can remember to say in a single assessment.

03

The family that's been showing up for years, quietly and completely, and is tired of having to prove it from scratch every time a plan review comes around.

For you

Your support network is
your most important care.

Living with a disability is a full time job. Managing your condition, navigating the NDIS, coordinating your supports, preparing for assessments. And somewhere in the middle of all of that, staying connected to the people who matter most to you.

The people who show up without being asked. Who notice when you're having a harder week. Who fill the gaps that formal supports never quite reach. Your mum. Your partner. Your neighbour who drives you on Tuesdays.

Your support network is probably the most important part of your care. And it's almost certainly the least documented.

When your I-CAN assessment arrives, the NDIS will want to understand what your people actually do for you. Not an estimate. Not a general description. A real picture of who does what, how often, and how much it takes.

Nobody should have to reconstruct that from memory.

Mallee checks in with your people, gently, twice a week, and builds a Care Record that captures what your support network actually does, in the language the NDIS understands, without you having to coordinate, remember, or chase anyone for information.

So when the assessment comes, you're ready. Not because you spent weeks preparing. Because Mallee has been quietly building the picture all along.

  • Your support network stays visible — your funding reflects reality
  • Months of documented evidence, not a single moment-in-time interview
  • No chasing paperwork, no reconstructing history, no drowning in the admin of your own condition
For your people

You didn't sign up
for the paperwork.

You love someone. You show up. You help with the things they can't do alone: the morning routine, the appointments, the phone calls, the worry. You do it because they matter to you. Not because anyone asked.

And somewhere along the way, it became a full time job that nobody sees.

The NDIS doesn't see it. Plan reviews don't capture it. And not once has anyone asked how you're doing with all of it.

Mallee sees you.

Two short conversations a week. We check in, we listen, and we turn what you do into the documented Care Record your family deserves. So when the I-CAN assessment arrives, you walk in with months of evidence. Not a single stressful memory test..

That feeling of having more on your plate than you can carry? It's not just you. It's 49% of people in your position. And it tends to get heavier before anyone asks if you need help.

Mallee asks. Every week.

  • Two minutes, twice a week. That's the entire ask.
  • What you do gets seen. Every hour, every act of care, on record.
  • Your wellbeing is monitored. Not just the participant's.
For NDIS families preparing for I-CAN

Your I-CAN assessment is coming.
Walk in with evidence, not estimates.

From mid-2026, every NDIS participant will go through a new support needs assessment. Section 34(1)(e) of the NDIS Act requires the NDIA to consider what is reasonable to expect from families and family networks. This means the assessor will ask about your family care arrangements. Mallee removes the stress of a moment-in-time interview, ensuring you can explain your support needs with months of structured evidence behind you.

Aligned with Australia's National Carer Strategy 2024–2034.

Built from personal experience

The feeling of always
being behind.

My Dad has Corticobasal Degeneration. Progressive conditions don't get better. The trajectory is fixed.. You stop spending your energy on paperwork and start spending it on the people you love.

What I kept hearing from families like mine was the same exhausting gap. There was always this feeling of being behind. Behind on the documentation, behind on the evidence,, never quite able to prove what everyone could plainly see.

Mallee exists so that families like mine don't lose each other to that feeling. So the care is seen, counted, and sustained.

— Sofia
How to access Mallee

Two ways to get started.
Both of them simple.

NDIS Funded
Replacement Support
$0 out of pocket / month

Fund Mallee through your NDIS plan using replacement support, the same mechanism that funds assistive technology. We guide you through the application at onboarding.

  • Two check-ins per week from day one
  • Full Care Record generated automatically
  • I-CAN domain mapping included
  • Pending Registration Group 0124
  • Your support coordinator can refer directly
Join Waitlist
Self Funded
Out of Pocket
$49 / month

Not yet on the NDIS, or prefer to get started straight away? Pay directly and begin building your Care Record today. No lock-in, cancel anytime.

  • Two check-ins per week from day one

  • Switch to NDIS funding at any time
  • Cancel anytime
